theme: plumbing a village above the permafrost
title: Plumbing the Village Above the Permafrost
strategies: flat-depth straight-line chain: connect buildings in input order via L-shaped segments, bury everything at 1.5x the worst possible cover-type depth (reproduces the evaluator's own naive baseline reference)
geometric nearest-neighbor MST (Manhattan distance, terrain never consulted while routing) plus a correctly-computed but uniform flat depth sized to the worst cover type the route happens to cross

warped-metric nearest-terminal Dijkstra Steiner heuristic: build the pipe network in the cost space where each cell weighs required_depth(cover_type)^2 (not 1), correctly identify that snow-holding ground's worst winter is a low-snow one rather than the coldest one, and bury each cell at exactly its own required depth
